summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the Mother's Day service with a warm greeting and a reading from 2 Corinthians 1:2-4, establishing the theme of divine comfort in trouble. He acknowledged that life is full of troubles and that people often turn to temporary, worldly comforts like substances, food, or relationships, which ultimately fail to solve the underlying problems. The core message was that God is the true and only source of all comfort. Pastor Travis, or PT, outlined four key truths from Scripture. First, God is the merciful Father and source of all comfort, as illustrated by Jesus's mission statement in Luke 4 and His invitation in Matthew 11. The Holy Spirit is our Comforter. Second, God comforts us with His peace, which the world cannot give, as demonstrated when Jesus calmed the storm in Mark 4 and as promised in Philippians 4. Third, God comforts us with His grace and mercy—unmerited favor and unconditional love—as described in Ephesians 2:8 and Ephesians 3:18, which speaks to the vast dimensions of God's love. Finally, God comforts us so that we can comfort others, turning our pain into purpose and ministry. PT powerfully illustrated this final point by sharing his personal testimony of suffering a hemorrhagic stroke six months prior, emphasizing how God can use our greatest hurts for His glory and to help others, referencing James 1:2-4. The call was to find true, lasting comfort in Jesus Christ and to extend the comfort we receive to those around us.
mainPoints: God is the merciful Father and the only true source of all comfort, not the temporary resources of this world.
God comforts us with His supernatural peace and grace, calming our storms and offering unmerited favor and unconditional love.
God comforts us in our troubles so that we can comfort others, using our pain and weaknesses to birth ministry and help others.

versesMentioned.text: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of compassion and the God of all comfort, who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.

The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners and recovery of sight for the blind, to set the oppressed free, to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor.

Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light.

A furious squall came up, and the waves broke over the boat, so that it was nearly swamped. Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on a cushion. The disciples woke him and said to him, 'Teacher, don’t you care if we drown?' He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves, 'Quiet! Be still!' Then the wind died down and it was completely calm.

Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith—and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God.
may have power, together with all the Lord’s holy people, to grasp how wide and long and high and deep is the love of Christ.

Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.

Monday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 1:3-4 (NIV) - 'Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of compassion and the God of all comfort, who comforts us in all our troubles...'
Monday.action: Identify one 'resource' (like work, a relationship, or a habit) you sometimes turn to for comfort instead of God. Intentionally pause and pray before engaging with it today.
Monday.prayer: Father, thank you for being my true source of comfort. Help me to turn to you first in every trouble today.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 11:28 (NIV) - 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.'
Tuesday.action: Find 10 minutes of quiet today. Don't scroll, watch, or eat. Simply sit in silence and consciously bring your weariness to Jesus.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, I come to you with my burdens. I receive the rest only you can give for my soul.

Wednesday.scripture: Mark 4:39 (NIV) - 'He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves, 'Quiet! Be still!' Then the wind died down and it was completely calm.'
Wednesday.action: Name one 'storm' (a worry, fear, or conflict) you are facing. Write it down, then write 'Peace, be still' next to it as a declaration of faith.
Wednesday.prayer: Lord, you have power over every storm. I speak your peace over [name your storm]. I trust you to bring calm.

Thursday.scripture: Ephesians 3:18 (NIV) - '...to grasp how wide and long and high and deep is the love of Christ.'
Thursday.action: Reflect on a past mistake or failure. Remind yourself, out loud, 'God's love for me is unconditional and reaches even here.'
Thursday.prayer: God, thank you for your limitless, unconditional love. Help me to receive it fully, especially where I feel unworthy.

Friday.scripture: Philippians 4:6-7 (NIV) - 'Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.'
Friday.action: Practice the Philippians 4 method with one specific anxiety today: pray about it, ask God for help, and thank Him for His faithfulness.
Friday.prayer: God, I present my request about [mention it] to you with thanksgiving. I receive your peace to guard my heart and mind.

Saturday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 1:4 (NIV) - '...so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.'
Saturday.action: Think of one person who might be struggling. Send them a simple, encouraging text or note, sharing a word of comfort or hope.
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, use the comfort you've given me to be a comfort to someone else today. Guide my words and actions.
